title: Hanging a pocket door kit
subtitle: Install a sliding cavity door on a proprietary steel track
excerpt: How to fit a pocket door using a proprietary steel track kit: setting the head track level, hanging the door on adjustable rollers, setting the door height and plumb, and fitting the floor guide and concealed handles.
sourceNotes: Newey and Drage Practical Carpentry and Joinery. Collins Complete DIY Manual. Cassell's Cyclopaedia of Mechanics.

glossaryTerms.definition: A door that slides sideways into a cavity within the wall frame when open, rather than swinging into the room. Requires either a proprietary steel track kit built into a new stud wall or a prepared cavity in an existing wall.
The horizontal top rail of a pocket door system, fixed to the wall studs or header above the opening. The door hangs from adjustable rollers that run along the inside of this track.

content.text: kit includes a steel stud frame that builds the wall cavity, a head track, and the roller hardware. The door itself is ordered or cut separately to the exact opening size. The critical steps are fixing the head track dead level and setting the roller height adjusters so the door hangs plumb; everything else follows from those two points.

A proprietary pocket door kit (frame, head track, rollers, floor guide, split door casing). A door leaf sized to suit the kit opening. 50 mm and 100 mm wood screws. Cordless drill, spirit level, tape measure, combination square, screwdriver set. A chisel and mallet if trimming existing frame timbers to accept the steel frame.
Step 1, build and fix the frame

Assemble the steel stud frame following the kit instructions and stand it in the opening. Check the frame is plumb on the face and square at the corners before driving screws into the floor and wall studs. The frame must be rigid; any racking at this stage transfers to the finished door.
Step 2, fit the head track and hang the door

to the top of the steel frame, checking it is level with the spirit level in both directions. Attach the roller brackets to the top edge of the door leaf at the positions specified in the kit instructions, then lift the door and hook the rollers into the track. The door should hang freely; adjust the roller height screws until the door face is plumb when checked with the spirit level on the leading edge.
Step 3, fit the floor guide, casing, and hardware

Fix the floor guide at the threshold to keep the bottom of the door from swinging out of the wall cavity. Fit the split door casing on both sides of the wall: the two halves are designed to sit flush and allow the door to pass between them. Fit the recessed pull handles and the magnetic or mechanical door stop inside the cavity. Slide the door slowly into the pocket and confirm it travels cleanly without rubbing at the top or bottom.

attrs.symptom: Door rubs along the top of the opening when sliding
attrs.cause: Head track was fixed slightly low, or the roller height has not been adjusted to compensate for the door weight.
attrs.fix: Raise the door on the roller height adjusters first; if the track itself is too low, refix it 5 to 10 mm higher.

attrs.symptom: Door swings outward as it slides into the pocket
attrs.cause: Floor guide is missing or misaligned, leaving the door bottom unsupported.
attrs.fix: Fit or reposition the floor guide centrally below the door track so it captures the bottom edge of the door leaf.
